name: mistune (Fast Python Markdown Parser)

performance.notes: Pure Python; fast regex-based parsing; good for real-time markdown rendering

agent_readiness.notes: Fast Python Markdown to HTML converter. import mistune. md = mistune.create_markdown(). html = md('# Hello *world*'). Plugins: from mistune.plugins import plugin_table, plugin_task_lists. md = mistune.create_markdown(plugins=['table', 'task_lists', 'strikethrough', 'footnotes']). Custom renderer: class MyRenderer(mistune.HTMLRenderer). mistune.create_markdown(renderer=MyRenderer()). Escape: create_markdown(escape=True). AST: mistune.create_markdown(renderer='ast'). XSS-safe with escape=True.

gotchas: mistune 3.x has breaking changes from 2.x API
escape=True required to prevent XSS from user-generated Markdown
CommonMark compliance is partial — edge cases may differ from spec
Plugin system changed in 3.x — old plugin code needs updating
best_when: Fast Markdown-to-HTML conversion in web applications
Custom rendering pipelines needing pluggable renderer
When AST access is needed for Markdown analysis
Lightweight Markdown parsing without heavy dependencies
avoid_when: Strict CommonMark compliance (use mistletoe or markdown-it-py)
summary: mistune is a fast Python Markdown-to-HTML converter. create_markdown(plugins=['table', 'task_lists']).html('# Hello'). Custom renderer subclassing. AST output with renderer='ast'. escape=True for XSS safety. BSD-3. mistune 3.x has breaking changes from 2.x. Good for web app Markdown rendering where speed matters. For strict CommonMark compliance use markdown-it-py. Always use escape=True for user-generated content.
